title: Deadly bombing of Iran primary school ‘a grave violation of humanitarian law’: UNESCO
content:
Deadly bombing of Iran primary school ‘a grave violation of humanitarian law’: UNESCO

Deadly bombing of Iran primary school ‘a grave violation of humanitarian law’: UNESCO

The UN education agency, UNESCO, says that the bombing of a primary school during the US and Israeli military attacks on Iran on Saturday constitutes a grave violation of humanitarian law.

Source: https://news.un.org/feed/view/en/story/2026/03/1167063...

Young people are increasingly affected by global events in this area. Gen Z and Gen Alpha seek clarity and constructive responses aligned with sustainable development and well-being (SDG 4: Quality Education).

Leaders from the United Spiritual Leaders Forum emphasize reflection and resilience in the face of uncertainty, in line with ethical frameworks that support youth well-being and global goals.

This story relates to SDG 4: Quality Education. The UNESCO tracks progress and supports initiatives in this area. Pearl News is an independent nonprofit and is not affiliated with the United Nations.

Constructive next steps and dialogue continue to shape how communities and youth engage with these challenges.
